test_cases.description: Workplace law podcast — full demographics, 2 sponsors, award nomination, 8.5K downloads/ep
test_cases.input_summary: Complete input: demographics, notable guests, social following, email list, sponsorship rates, existing sponsors, award
test_cases.output_summary: Full media kit with 7 sections; audience section goes beyond demographics to include behavioral signal (action rate); stats presented as clean table; sponsorship packages as labeled table; social proof section concise; host bio credible

test_cases.description: New podcast — 3 months old, no demographic data, no sponsors yet, 800 downloads/ep
test_cases.input_summary: Early-stage show, limited data, no sponsors, no press, seeking first sponsor outreach
test_cases.output_summary: Skill correctly emphasizes niche and audience quality over volume; listener count stated honestly without inflation; sections that lack data noted as 'in development' rather than fabricated; packages framed as introductory rates

test_cases.description: True crime podcast — press mentions, no demographic data available, seeking award submission
test_cases.input_summary: Established show, strong press coverage, seeking award/directory submission format rather than sponsor-facing document
test_cases.output_summary: Format adapts slightly for award submission context — editorial voice is stronger, press quotes prominent, sponsorship section de-emphasized; format still works for both use cases

notes: The skill handles sparse inputs gracefully — it does not invent data that hasn't been provided, which is critical for credibility. The strongest outputs come when the user provides demographic and behavioral data.
